title: Microbial cfDNA Sample Processing and Extraction Protocol
author: Martin Schou Pedersen

steps.instruction: This starting point is based on the assumption that samples arrive in in Streck Cell-Free DNA BCT tubes.
Centrifuge the sample tubes at 1,600 × g (RCF) for 20 minutes.
Pipette plasma into 2 mL LoBind tubes, maximum 1 mL per tube. Centrifuge at 15,595 × g (RCF) for 10 minutes. Adding more than 1 mL per tube will result in suboptimal pelleting of cells.
Centrifuge tubes at 200 × g for 30 seconds to collect liquid from the cap.
Incubate tubes at room temperature (15–25°C) for 10 minutes with continuous mixing (invert/tilt to prevent beads from settling).
For each sample, prepare 2 LoBind tubes each containing: 1.5 mL, Plasma 45 µL, Magnetic Bead Suspension 82 µL, Proteinase K 225 µL, Bead Binding Buffer
Allocate 3 mL plasma per sample for extraction. Pipette residual plasma (not needed for extraction) into 2 mL LoBind tubes to store in −80°C freezer. Expected volume: 4–6 mL plasma per Streck tube.
Place the tube on the magnet for >1 minute until liquid is clear. Transfer supernatant to a new Bead Elution tube. Turn on the Thermomixer and set to 56°C now so it is ready for later.
Transfer contents to a new Bead Elution tube. Place on Thermomixer at 300 rpm for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Remove tube from magnet. Add 200 µL Bead Elution Buffer and mix by inversion.
Add the liquid from the second tube for the same patient to the emptied tube on the magnet. Wait 1 minute until the liquid is clear. Remove supernatant.
Place one tube per patient on the magnet for >1 minute until liquid is clear. Remove supernatant.
Prepare one column per sample with a 2 mL collection tube. Load the supernatant onto column. Centrifuge at 6,000 × g for 1 minute. Transfer column to a new 2 mL collection tube.
Add 300 µL Buffer ACB to the tube. Vortex for 10 seconds. Centrifuge briefly to remove liquid from cap.
Add 50 µL nuclease-free water to the center of the column membrane. Incubate for 1 minute at room temperature.
Incubate on a Thermomixer at 56°C for 3 minutes with the cap open.
Centrifuge at 15,595 × g for 3 minutes. Transfer the column to a new 1.5 mL Bead Elution tube.
Add 500 µL Buffer ACW2 to column. Centrifuge at 6,000 × g for 1 minute. Transfer the column to a new 2 mL collection tube.
Store the cfDNA on ice until ready library preparation. If not proceeding to library prep on the same day, store cfDNA at −80°C.
Centrifuge at 15,595 × g for 1 minute.

Measure the DNA concentration with Qubit. Expected yield: 15–40 ng total. For guidance please refer to: Josh Quick 2025. DNA Quantification using the Qubit Fluorometer. protocols.iohttps://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.261gekdeyg47/v1
Centrifuge at 15,595 × g for 1 minute. Collect the eluate (~50 µL).
Return the 50 µL eluate to the center of the column membrane. Incubate for 1 minute at room temperature.

After completing cfDNA extraction and quality measurement (Qubit), continue with the following protocol for end repair, barcode ligation, adaptor ligation, flow cell loading, and sequencing Martin Schou Pedersen 2026. Microbial cfDNA Library Preparation and Sequencing with Oxford Nanopore. protocols.iohttps://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x54v99e84v3e/v1
